title: Linked multiple blockchain system
abstract: Embodiments of the disclosure are directed to a real-time payments settlement system based on distributed ledgers, which may be implemented with blockchain technology. The distributed ledgers may be separate and distinct and each ledger can be used to track, record, and settle transactions between users of a pool account. The ledgers may also be interconnected with one another by being connected to a main ledger, and this network of ledgers can be used to track, record, and facilitate settlement of transactions between users of different pool accounts that are associated with different ledgers. This provides redundancy, transparency, and data access controls, while enabling multicurrency, cross-border transactions to be quickly verified, performed, and settled on a per-transaction basis while reducing the capital requirements of its users.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A method comprising: receiving, at a node of a first plurality of nodes hosting a first blockchain ledger, a first transaction, wherein each user of a first set of users deposited funds for settlement into a first pool account, wherein the first transaction is from a first user to a second user for a first amount, and wherein the first blockchain ledger records transactions between the first set of users including the first user and the second user; verifying, by the node of the first plurality of nodes, that the first transaction is capable of taking place by checking the first blockchain ledger; and updating, by the node of the first plurality of nodes, the first blockchain ledger with the first transaction to include a block with data relating to the first transaction. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the first blockchain ledger further records, for each transaction between the first set of users: a payer; a payee; and a transaction amount. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein verifying that the transaction is capable of taking place comprises checking the first blockchain ledger for a history of transactions associated with the first user. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein verifying that the first transaction is capable of taking place comprises checking an account value associated with the first user. 5. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: receiving, at the node of the first plurality of nodes, a second transaction between the first user to a third user that did not deposit funds for settlement into the first pool account; verifying, by the node of the first plurality of nodes, that the second transaction is capable of taking place by checking the first blockchain ledger; and 

 communicating, by the node of the first plurality of nodes to a second plurality of nodes hosting a second blockchain ledger, the second transaction. 6. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: determining, by the node of the first plurality of nodes, that the first transaction reduces an account value associated with the first user below a first threshold; and sending, by the node of the first plurality of nodes, a communication to the first user to deposit additional funds into the pool account. 7. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: determining, by the node of the first plurality of nodes, that the first transaction increases an account value associated with the second user above a second threshold; and sending, by the node of the first plurality of nodes, a communication to the second user to withdraw funds from the pool account. 8. A computing system comprising: a processor; a computer-readable memory containing program instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to: receive, a first transaction from a first user to a second user for a first amount; verify that the first transaction is capable of taking place by checking a first blockchain ledger, wherein the first blockchain ledger records transactions between a first set of users including the first user and the second user, and wherein each user of the first set of users deposited funds for settlement into a first pool account; and updating the first blockchain ledger with the first transaction to include a block with data relating to the first transaction. 9. The computing system of claim 8, wherein the first blockchain ledger further records, for each transaction between the first set of users: a payer; 

 a payee; and a transaction amount. 10. The computing system of claim 8, wherein verifying that the first transaction is capable of taking place comprises checking the first blockchain ledger for a history of transactions associated with the first user. 1 1 . The computing system of claim 8, wherein verifying that the first transaction is capable of taking place comprises checking an account value associated with the first user. 12. The computing system of claim 8, wherein the program instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the processor to: receive a second transaction between the first user to a third user that did not deposit funds for settlement into the first pool account; verify that the second transaction is capable of taking place by checking the first blockchain ledger; and communicate the second transaction to a second plurality of nodes hosting a second blockchain ledger. 13. The computing system of claim 8, wherein the program instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the processor to: determine that the first transaction reduces an account value associated with the first user below a first threshold; and sending a communication to the first user to deposit additional funds into the pool account. 14. The computing system of claim 8, wherein the program instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the processor to: determine that the first transaction increases an account value associated with the second user above a second threshold; and send a communication to the second user to withdraw funds from the pool account. 15. A method comprising: 

 receiving a transaction between a first user to a second user for a first amount, wherein the first user deposited funds for settlement into a first pool account and the second user deposited funds for settlement into a second pool account; verifying that the transaction is capable of taking place by checking an account value associated with the first user at the first pool account; sending, to a first plurality of nodes hosting a first blockchain ledger, a first communication, wherein the first blockchain ledger records transactions between users that deposited funds for settlement into the first pool account, and wherein the first communication instructs the first plurality of nodes to update the first blockchain ledger to include a decrease in the account value associated with the first user at the first pool account by the first amount; and sending, to a second plurality of nodes hosting a second blockchain ledger, a second communication, wherein the second blockchain ledger records transactions between users that deposited funds for settlement into the second pool account, and wherein the second communication instructs the second plurality of nodes to update the second blockchain ledger to include an increase in an account value associated with the second user at the second pool account. 16. The method of claim 15, further comprising: determining a currency associated with the second pool account; and converting the first amount into a second amount based on the determined currency, and wherein the second communication further instructs the second plurality of nodes to update the second blockchain ledger to include the increase in the account value associated with the second user at the second pool account by the second amount. 17. The method of claim 15, wherein the first communication further instructs the first plurality of nodes to update the first blockchain ledger to include a corresponding increase in a currency account value at the first pool account by the first amount. 18. The method of claim 16, wherein the second communication further instructs the second plurality of nodes to update the second blockchain ledger 

 to include a corresponding decrease in a currency account value at the second pool account by the second amount. 19. The method of claim 17, further comprising: receiving a communication that the currency account value at the first pool account is above a first threshold; and withdrawing funds from the currency account at the first pool account to bring the currency account value below the first threshold. 20. The method of claim 18, further comprising: receiving a communication that the currency account value at the second pool account is below a second threshold; and depositing funds into the currency account at the second pool account to bring the currency account value above the second threshold. 21 . The method of claim 15, wherein the first blockchain ledger is a private permission based ledger, and wherein verifying that the transaction is capable of taking place further includes: checking, based on having a data access permission, the first blockchain ledger for the account value associated with the first user at the first pool account; checking the transaction against a first set of rules associated with the first blockchain ledger, the first of rules including rules configured by the first user and integrated with the first blockchain ledger; and checking the transaction against a second set of rules associated with the second blockchain ledger, the second of rules including rules configured by the second user and integrated with the second blockchain ledger. 22. A computing system comprising: a processor; a computer-readable memory containing program instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to: receive a transaction between a first user to a second user for a first amount, wherein the first user deposited funds for settlement into a first 

 pool account and the second user deposited funds for settlement into a second pool account; verify that the transaction is capable of taking place by checking an account value associated with the first user at the first pool account; send, to a first plurality of nodes hosting a first blockchain ledger, a first communication, wherein the first blockchain ledger records transactions between users that deposited funds for settlement into the first pool account, and wherein the first communication instructs the first plurality of nodes to update the first blockchain ledger to include a decrease in the account value associated with the first user at the first pool account by the first amount; and send, to a second plurality of nodes hosting a second blockchain ledger, a second communication, wherein the second blockchain ledger records transactions between users that deposited funds for settlement into the second pool account, and wherein the second communication instructs the second plurality of nodes to update the second blockchain ledger to include an increase in an account value associated with the second user at the second pool account. 23. The computing system of claim 22, wherein the program instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the processor to: determine a currency associated with the second pool account; and convert the first amount into a second amount based on the determined currency, and wherein the second communication further instructs the second plurality of nodes to update the second blockchain ledger to include the increase in the account value associated with the second user at the second pool account by the second amount. 24. The computing system of claim 22, wherein the first communication further instructs the first plurality of nodes to update the first blockchain ledger to include a corresponding increase in a currency account value at the first pool account by the first amount. 25. The computing system of claim 23, wherein the second communication further instructs the second plurality of nodes to update the second blockchain ledger to include a corresponding decrease in a currency account value at the second pool account by the second amount. 26. The computing system of claim 22, wherein the program instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the processor to: receive a communication that the currency account value at the first pool account is above a first threshold; and withdraw funds from the currency account at the first pool account to bring the currency account value below the first threshold.
